name: DIVERSIFIED HEALTHCARE TRUST
business: Diversified Healthcare Trust is a REIT that primarily owns senior living communities, medical office and life science properties, and other healthcare-related properties in the United States. It generates revenue from resident fees, rental income, and property management. The company leases nearly all of its senior living communities to its taxable REIT subsidiaries and uses third-party managers to operate them.
sellsTo: Seniors (independent living, assisted living, memory care, skilled nursing)
namedCustomers: The University of Kansas Health System

exposures.sentence: Our SHOP segment consists of managed senior living communities that provide short term and long term residential living and in some instances care and other services for residents where we pay fees to managers to operate the communities on our behalf.

Our Medical Office and Life Science Portfolio consists of commercial properties constructed for use or operated as medical office space for physicians and other healthcare personnel and other businesses in medical related fields, including clinics and life science or laboratory uses.

Our Medical Office and Life Science Portfolio segment primarily consists of medical office properties leased to medical providers and other medical related businesses, as well as life science properties primarily leased to biotech laboratories and other similar tenants.

catalysts.event: Completed transition of all Five Star managed senior living communities to third-party managers
catalysts.sentence: As of December 31, 2025, we completed the transition of all of the Five Star managed senior living communities to these managers.

catalysts.event: AlerisLife paid aggregate cash dividend of $80.0 million
catalysts.sentence: On January 9, 2026, AlerisLife paid an aggregate cash dividend of $80.0 million to its stockholders.

catalysts.event: Notice to exercise purchase option for two finance lease properties, closing expected in April 2026
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we provided notice to exercise our purchase option for the two properties securing our finance leases for $14,500, with closing expected in April 2026.

catalysts.event: 13 properties under agreement to sell for aggregate sales price of $23 million
catalysts.sentence: As of February 20, 2026, we had 13 properties under agreement to sell for an aggregate sales price of $23,000, excluding closing costs.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. healthcare market represents approximately 18% of U.S. GDP
tamClaims.sentence: The healthcare industry remains one of the most resilient commercial real estate sectors, in part due to the scale of the U.S. healthcare market, which collectively represents approximately 18% of the U.S. gross domestic product, or GDP, according to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, or CMS.

tamClaims.claim: Healthcare spending projected to grow at 5.8% per year, exceeding 20% of GDP by 2033
tamClaims.sentence: CMS reports that healthcare spending is projected to grow at an average rate of 5.8% per year, and as a result, health spending as a percentage of GDP is projected to exceed 20% by 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Age 75+ demographic projected to grow at 4% annually between 2025 and 2035
tamClaims.sentence: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, the age 75+ demographic is projected to be among the fastest growing age cohorts in the United States with an average annual growth of 4% between 2025 and 2035.

tamClaims.claim: Annual inventory growth for senior living was 0.5% in Q4 2025
tamClaims.sentence: According to The National Investment Center for Seniors Housing and Care, or NIC, annual inventory growth was 0.5% across primary and secondary markets during the fourth quarter of 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Annual absorption for senior living was 2.8% in Q4 2025
tamClaims.sentence: Additionally, annual absorption was 2.8% for the fourth quarter of 2025, according to NIC.
